channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: Tesla's Ambitious Robotaxi Plan: Challenges Ahead for Driverless Ride-Hailing Services
item.description: Tesla CEO Elon Musk promises driverless ride-hailing services in California and Texas next year, but regulatory and technical hurdles loom large.
item.dc:creator: Reuters, Fast Company

item.title: House Republican presses Meta on censoring political content
item.description: Texas Rep. August Pfluger (R) expressed concern in a letter to Meta on Tuesday over the platform's decision to automatically opt users out of political content, arguing the platform's users should be able to make that decision for themselves.
item.dc:creator: Juliann Ventura, The Hill

item.title: F5, Nvidia team to boost AI, cloud security
item.description: F5 and Nvidia are expanding their partnership to help enterprises build AI infrastructure and bolster cloud-based application security.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: White House tells intelligence agencies: Use more AI
item.description: In a first, US President Joseph R. Biden Jr. issued a national security memorandum today telling federal intelligence agencies they need to pilot and deploy artificial intelligence (AI) in an effort to boost the nation’s security.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Montana AG sues TikTok, alleging platform is addictive, harmful to youth mental health
item.description: Montana Attorney General Austin Knudsen filed a lawsuit Thursday against TikTok, alleging the social media platform knowingly shared addictive and harmful content with children and teens.
item.dc:creator: Miranda Nazzaro, The Hill

item.title: AI-powered weapons scanners used in NYC subway found zero guns in one month test
item.description: A pilot program testing AI-powered weapons scanners inside some New York City subway stations this summer did not detect any passengers with firearms — but falsely alerted more than 100 times.
item.dc:creator: Jake Offenhartz, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Google's partnership with AI startup Anthropic faces a UK competition investigation
item.description: LONDON (AP) — Britain’s competition watchdog said Thursday it's opening a formal investigation into Google’s partnership with artificial intelligence startup Anthropic.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Russia spread false claims about Helene, Milton, government response: Report
item.description: Russia spread false claims about the U.S. government’s response to hurricanes Helene and Milton in an effort to sow discontent and undermine public support for Ukraine, according to a new report from the Institute for Strategic Dialogue (ISD).
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, The Hill

item.title: Nigeria releases American crypto executive after dropping money laundering case
item.description: An American cryptocurrency executive held in Nigeria for the past eight months has been released after authorities there announced that they were ending his money laundering trial on health and diplomatic grounds.
item.dc:creator: Chinedu Asadu And Eric Tucker, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Woman says teen son died by suicide amid relationship with AI chatbot
item.description: Sewell Setzer III, died by suicide after a monthslong, “hypersexualized” relationship with an AI character, his mother said in a federal lawsuit.
item.dc:creator: Elizabeth Prann, NewsNation

item.title: Larry Magid: Summit at Oxford focuses on Generative AI regulations
item.description: Although there were expected disagreements, I was left with a strong sense of hope in some shared values
item.dc:creator: Larry Magid, The Mercury News

item.title: Marc Benioff rails against Microsoft’s copilot
item.description: After lashing out against Microsoft at the Dreamforce customer conference, Salesforce CEO Marc Benioff has taken another swipe at his competitor. “When you look at how Copilot has been delivered to customers, it’s disappointing,” Benioff wrote in a post on Twitter/X.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Russia amplified hurricane disinformation to drive Americans apart, researchers find
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Russia has helped amplify and spread false and misleading internet claims about recent hurricanes in the United States and the federal government's response, part of a wider effort by the Kremlin to manipulate America's political discourse before the presidential election, new rese
item.dc:creator: DAVID KLEPPER, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Dulling the impact of AI-fueled cyber threats with AI
item.description: IT leaders are placing faith in AI. Consider 76 percent of IT leaders believe that generative AI (GenAI) will significantly impact their organizations, with 76 percent increasing their budgets to pursue AI. But when it comes to cybersecurity, AI has become a double-edged sword.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: New rules for US national security agencies balance AI's promise with need to protect against risks
item.description: New rules from the White House on the use of artificial intelligence by U.S. national security and spy agencies aim to balance the technology’s promise with the need to protect against its risks.
item.dc:creator: David Klepper, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Sólo el 48% de las iniciativas digitales cumplen o superan los objetivos empresariales
item.description: De media, únicamente el 48% de las iniciativas digitales en toda la empresa cumplen o superan los objetivos de resultados de negocio.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Georgia, candidates target of ‘sustained’ cyberattacks: Reports
item.description: CNN says Georgia fended off the likely overseas attack, while Microsoft says China, Russia and Iran are targeting the U.S. election process.
item.dc:creator: Rich Johnson, NewsNation

item.title: White House reviewing HIPAA cybersecurity updates
item.description: HHS wants to boost the cybersecurity protections required under HIPAA, as ransomware groups continue to target sensitive healthcare information. The post White House reviewing HIPAA cybersecurity updates first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Justin Doubleday, Federal News Network

item.title: Two new HHS programs to help with complicated health challenges
item.description: "A lot of the efforts that we're doing across the government have a plug into the artificial intelligence world," said Andy Kilianski. The post Two new HHS programs to help with complicated health challenges first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Tom Temin, Federal News Network

item.title: Elon Musk says he’ll fix the government under Trump. His track record paints a different picture
item.description: Elon Musk is making some big promises while campaigning for former President Donald Trump. But his business record calls into question whether he can deliver.
item.dc:creator: CNN.com, The Mercury News

item.title: Biden administration to provide $325 million for new Michigan semiconductor factory
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Biden administration said Monday that it would provide up to $325 million to Hemlock Semiconductor for a new factory, a move that could help give Democrats a political edge in the swing state of Michigan ahead of election day.
item.dc:creator: JOSH BOAK, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Microsoft to launch autonomous AI agents in November
item.description: Microsoft will soon let customers build autonomous AI agents that can be configured to perform complex tasks with little or no input from humans.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Slack researcher discusses the fear, loathing and excitement surrounding AI in the workplace
item.description: SAN FRANCISCO (AP) — Artificial intelligence's recent rise to the forefront of business has left most office workers wondering how often they should use the technology and whether a computer will eventually replace them.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL LIEDTKE, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Floating energy platform could change way you get power in future
item.description: Tech expert Kurt “CyberGuy" Knutsson explains how NoviOcean's hybrid platform uses wind, solar and wave power to generate clean energy.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: How Kubecost shines a light on GPU efficiency
item.description: Many enterprise engineering teams—perhaps most at this point—have dived headlong into GPUs to build out proofs-of-concept and operationalize new products. While competitive pressures dictated the direction, GPU cost concerns have begun moving from the back burner to the front.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Over 600M cyberattacks target Windows users every day
item.description: Windows operating system users face more than 600 million cyberattacks every day, according to Kurt "CyberGuy" Knutsson. The attacks are on the rise.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Rising crypto scams leave Americans reeling from billions in losses
item.description: Scammers have illustrated that cryptocurrency is not as foolproof as it has promised. Americans lost $5.6 billion to crypto scams in 2023, according to the FBI.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: October is Cybersecurity Awareness Month. Here's how to stay safe from scams
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — October is Cybersecurity Awareness Month, which means it's the perfect time to learn how to protect yourself from scams. “Scams have become so sophisticated now.
item.dc:creator: ADRIANA MORGA, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Chinese humanoid robot could be the future of affordable in-home care
item.description: A humanoid robot made by a Chinese company proposes to redefine personalized elderly care and assistance for people with disabilities.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Judge delays order in antitrust case requiring Google to open up its app store
item.description: SAN FRANCISCO (AP) — A federal judge on Friday delayed an order requiring Google to open up its Android app store to more competition until an appeals court decides whether to block the shake-up because of legal questions surrounding a jury's verdict that branded Google as an illegal monopolist.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL LIEDTKE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC
